$comment: Maps logical model tiers (used in subagent `model:` frontmatter) to concrete model IDs. This indirection is what makes the harness portable to non-Anthropic / local models: under a LiteLLM gateway (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL → proxy), override the tiers below to point at whatever is available. The `model:` field in an agent is a LOGICAL TIER, not a hard model ID.

roles.$comment: Role → tier. Lets routing express intent (planning wants the strongest model; bulk coding can be cheaper/local) independent of which concrete model fills each tier.

local_fallback_example.$comment: Reference config for the planner=remote / coder=local split. Opus plans and verifies via the Anthropic API; a local model (served by Ollama/vLLM behind a LiteLLM proxy) does the coding/bulk work. Copy these over `default` in a per-deployment override to activate. Model IDs are illustrative — set to whatever your gateway serves.
